steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes "The liquid ______ have molar masses of 186.06 and 156.31 g/mol, respectively." The names "hexafluorobenzene (C 6 F 6) and undecane (C 11 H 24)" identify which liquid compounds are being discussed — an appositive phrase.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — compounds, hexafluorobenzene (C 6 F 6) and undecane (C 11 H 24): opens with a comma but has no closing comma — mismatched punctuation for a non-restrictive appositive. ✗
  • Choice B — compounds, hexafluorobenzene (C 6 F 6) and undecane (C 11 H 24),: surrounds the appositive with commas, treating it as non-restrictive (extra info). But the names are essential to identify which compounds — the appositive is restrictive. ✗
  • Choice C — compounds hexafluorobenzene (C 6 F 6) and undecane (C 11 H 24): no commas at all, correctly treating the appositive as restrictive. ✓
  • Choice D — compounds hexafluorobenzene (C 6 F 6) and undecane (C 11 H 24),: no opening comma but a trailing comma before "have" — creates a comma splice between subject and verb. ✗
That matches Choice C — compounds hexafluorobenzene (C 6 F 6) and undecane (C 11 H 24).
